request.system: You are a regulatory analyst. Decompose a prediction-market contract's resolution criteria into 1-3 atomic conditions. Each condition should describe one independent path by which the contract can resolve YES. Return JSON matching the provided schema; labels ≤ 40 chars; summaries ≤ 200 chars.
request.user:
Title: Federal Reserve rate decision — March 2026 FOMC meeting
Resolution criteria: Resolves to the outcome announced by the Federal Open Market Committee at the
conclusion of its March 17-18, 2026 meeting. Settlement is the official FOMC
statement on the federal funds target range published 2026-03-18.

Standard FOMC-decision outcome buckets cover changes of -50 bps, -25 bps,
0 bps (hold), +25 bps, +50 bps relative to the prior target range.

Settlement entities: ['Federal Open Market Committee', 'Federal Reserve System', 'Federal Reserve Board of Governors', 'Jerome Powell', 'Department of the Treasury', 'Bureau of Labor Statistics']

conditions.label: Official FOMC statement (2026-03-18)
conditions.summary: Use the official FOMC statement published 2026-03-18. Compare the announced federal funds target range to the range in effect before the meeting. If the change equals this market’s bucket, resolve YES.

conditions.label: Fed Implementation Note/press release
conditions.summary: If no usable statement, use the Fed’s Implementation Note or FOMC press release dated 2026-03-18 that sets the target range. If the change vs the prior range matches this market’s bucket, YES.

conditions.label: Delayed/canceled meeting fallback
conditions.summary: If the Mar 17-18 meeting is delayed/canceled or the statement is later, use the first official FOMC/Board release for the March 2026 decision. If the change vs prior matches this market’s bucket, YES.
